s: antemano means 'beforehand, in advance' in Spanish
e: Antemano refers to something done or decided beforehand or in advance. From the example: 'la decisión estaba tomada de antemano' (the decision was made in advance).

Consejero refers to a person who provides advice or counsel, often in an official capacity. From the example: 'agradezco al consejero por esta información útil' (I thank the counselor for this useful information).

Inexplicable means something that cannot be explained or understood. From the example: 'intenta también explicar y conceptualizar lo inexplicable' (it also attempts to explain and conceptualize the unexplainable).

Soltero describes a person who is unmarried, not in a relationship, or specifically an adult male who has never married. From the example: 'cuando era soltero le gustaba salir con chicas' (when he was single, he liked to go out with girls).
Subasta refers to a public sale where items are sold to the highest bidder. From the example: 'procuró comprarse un coche en una subasta' (he managed to buy a car at an auction).

s: ficha means 'index card, chip, token' in Spanish
e: Ficha refers to a small card, piece, or token used for identification, organization, or games. From the example: 'Está clasificando fichas con recetas en casillero' (He is sorting index cards with recipes into a box).

s: This Spanish adjective means something that is trivial or of little importance or consequence.
q: What Spanish word is related to "significant"?
e: Insignificante means something that is not important, trivial, or of little consequence. From the example: 'por este servicio cobramos un insignificante dos por ciento' (we charge an insignificant 2 percent for this service).

Meridional refers to something located in the south or relating to southern regions. From the example: 'el río Paraguay es la más meridional y diminuta' (the River Paraguay is the smallest and southernmost river).

Textura refers to the way a surface feels or looks, particularly with regard to smoothness, roughness, or patterns. From the example: 'Nos ha sorprendido mucho su textura satinada' (We were very surprised by its satiny texture).

Merced refers to a favor, mercy, or compassionate act. Often used in the phrase 'a merced de' meaning 'at the mercy of'. From the example: 'la población civil estaría a merced de sus atacantes' (the civil population would be at the mercy of its attackers).

s: rascar means 'to scratch, scrape' in Spanish
e: Rascar means to scratch or scrape a surface with one's fingernails or another object. From the example: 'yo le rascaba la espalda con las uñas' (I used to scratch his back with my nails).

s: desenvolver means 'to clear up, unwrap' in Spanish
e: Desenvolver means to unwrap something or to clear up confusion. From the example: 'si yo pudiera desenvolver mis pensamientos me sentiría mejor' (if I could clear up my thoughts I would feel better).

Puntual means arriving or doing something exactly at the scheduled time or on time. From the example: 'la oficial es muy puntual y no podían hacerlos esperar' (the officer is very punctual and could not make them wait).

Nicaragüense refers to someone or something from Nicaragua, the Central American country. From the example: 'un símbolo de la historia nicaragüense del siglo XX' (a symbol of the Nicaraguan history of the 20th century).

s: requerimiento means 'requirement, request, summons' in Spanish
e: Requerimiento refers to a requirement, formal request, or legal summons. From the example: 'sólo se llega a ofrecer a los estudiantes tras muchos requerimientos' (it is only offered to students after many difficult requirements).

s: This Spanish adjective describes something of middling or moderate quality, not particularly good.
q: What Spanish word is related to "excellent"?
e: Mediocre means something that is of middling or moderate quality, not very good or impressive. From the example: 'no eres un ser mediocre, eres una gran artista' (you are not a mediocre being, you are a great artist).

Rienda refers to leather straps used to control a horse, or metaphorically, restraint or control. Used in the phrase 'dar rienda suelta' meaning to give free rein. From the example: 'la caza le permite dar rienda suelta a cierta agresividad latente' (hunting allows him to give free rein to some of his latent aggression).

s: derribar means 'to knock down, overthrow' in Spanish
e: Derribar means to knock something or someone down forcefully, or to overthrow a government or regime. From the example: 'un golpe lo derribó por el suelo' (a blow knocked him down to the floor).

Compacto means something that is tightly packed, dense, or taking up little space. From the example: 'yo sé viajar, mi equipaje está siempre bien compacto' (I know how to travel, my luggage is always very compact).

Teclado refers to a keyboard, either on a computer, piano, or other device with keys. From the example: 'es fácil usa las funcionas táctiles, el mouse y el teclado' (it's easy to use the touch functions, the mouse, and the keyboard).

s: tanque means 'tank, large container' in Spanish
e: Tanque refers to a large container for storing liquids or a military tank. From the example: 'las tuberías y tanques de refinerías de petróleo' (the pipes and the tanks of oil refineries).

Helicóptero refers to an aircraft with rotating propeller blades on top that allow it to hover and move in any direction. From the example: 'El primer helicóptero que voló no tenía motor para las helices' (The first helicopter flew without a motor for the propellers).

Envidiar means to feel jealousy or discontent over someone else's possessions, qualities, or luck. From the example: 'les admiraba, les envidiaba, les codiciaba' (she admired, envied, and coveted them).

s: capacitar means 'to prepare, train' in Spanish
e: Capacitar means to train or prepare someone with the necessary skills or knowledge for a task or profession. From the example: 'deben terminar sus estudios y capacitarse en un trabajo productiva' (they should finish their studies and get trained in some kind of productive work).
Aflojar means to make something less tight or to relax tension. From the example: 'eso sirvió para aflojar mis tensiones' (that served to relieve my stress).
Maldad refers to evil, wickedness, or immoral conduct. From the example: 'la maldad de Satanás no concluyó ahí' (the wickedness of Satan did not stop there).

s: brusco means 'brusque, abrupt, sudden' in Spanish
e: Brusco means characterized by sudden, sharp, or rude manner. From the example: 'le dio la espalda con un brusco movimiento' (she turned her back on him with an abrupt movement).

s: envejecer means 'to age, grow old' in Spanish
e: Envejecer means to become or make someone old or aged. From the example: 'haber aceptado que envejecer es la única alternativa a la muerte' (having accepted that aging is the only alternative to death).

s: estrechar means 'to tighten, make closer' in Spanish
e: Estrechar means to tighten, make narrower, or to draw closer emotionally or physically. From the example: 'el objetivo es estrechar la comunicación y mantener contactos frecuentes' (we should increase communication and maintain frequent contacts).

s: cagar means 'to dirty, crap' in Spanish
e: Cagar is a vulgar verb meaning to defecate, often used figuratively to mean ruining something or doing someone wrong. From the example: 'reflexionen sobre toda la gente a la que cagaron . . . que son muchos' (think about all of the people they screwed over . . . and there's a lot of them).

Atleta refers to a person who participates in sports and athletics, especially at competitive levels. From the example: 'Las atletas estadounidenses ganaron la mayoría de las medallas' (The American athletes won the majority of the medals).

s: reformar means 'to reform, renovate' in Spanish
e: Reformar means to change something for the better, to renovate or remodel a structure, or to improve a system. From the example: 'dijo que pedirá poderes especiales al parlamento para reformar leyes' (he said he will ask for special powers from parliament to reform laws).
Suplicar means to beg earnestly or plead with someone to do something. From the example: 'Te suplico me ayudes a vencer las dificultades' (I beg you to help me overcome my problems).

Desconfianza means lack of trust or confidence in someone or something. From the example: 'hay desconfianza en la fortaleza del dólar ahora' (there is a lack of confidence in the strength of the dollar right now).

Forzado means something that is forced, unnatural, or done with effort. From the example: 'La relación entre los dos me ha parecido forzada y artificial' (The relationship between the two of them seems forced and artificial to me).

Vasco refers to the Basque people, region, culture, or language of the Basque Country in northern Spain. From the example: 'están incorporando La Rioja y las tierras vascas occidentales' (they are including La Rioja and the western Basque lands).

Resurrección refers to the act of returning to life after death, particularly in religious contexts. From the example: 'se celebra el primer día de la semana desde la resurrección de Cristo' (they have celebrated the first day of the week since the resurrection of Christ).

s: This Spanish adjective describes something of great size, significance, or historical importance.
q: What Spanish word is related to "small"?
e: Monumental means something of great size, importance, or significance. From the example: 'esta es la monumental iglesia de Nuestra Señora' (this is the monumental church of Nuestra Señora).

s: trascendencia means 'transcendent nature, significance' in Spanish
e: Trascendencia refers to great importance, significance, or the quality of transcending ordinary limits. From the example: 'su trascendencia artística es indudable' (his artistic importance is undisputed).

Ecuación refers to a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are equal. From the example: 'los algoritmos resuelven una ecuación o ejecutan una formula' (algorithms solve equations and execute formulas).
Seriamente means in a serious manner, with gravity and sincerity. From the example: 'voy a hablar seriamente con ella' (I am going to speak seriously with her).

Secuestro refers to the illegal taking of a person by force or the seizing of property. From the example: 'está preocupado por la posibilidad de secuestro de su hijo' (she's worried about the possible kidnapping of her child).
Apuro refers to a difficult situation, predicament, or trouble that one finds themselves in. From the example: 'Estuve en un apuro financiero' (I was in financial trouble).
Nulo means something that is invalid, void, or of no value or effect. From the example: 'el voto nulo no cuenta en las elecciones' (absentee voting does not count in the elections).

s: quehacer means 'task, job, chore' in Spanish
e: Quehacer refers to a task, job, or household chore that needs to be done. From the example: 'es para los quehaceres domésticos: cocinando, lavando y limpiando' (it's for doing household chores: cooking, washing, and cleaning).
Amparo refers to protection, shelter, or refuge from danger or hardship. From the example: 'es mejor que busque amparo antes de anochecer' (it is better that you look for shelter before nightfall).

Irónico means expressing irony or containing irony, often characterized by mocking or saying something opposite to what is meant. From the example: 'ella dejó escurrir un comentario irónico' (she let slip an ironic comment).

Burbuja refers to a bubble, typically of gas or air enclosed in a liquid or solid. From the example: 'aunque se hubiese creado una burbuja de aire cuando el navío zozobró' (even though an air bubble had been created when the ship went down).

s: 'insertar' means 'to insert, put in' in Spanish
e: Insertar is a Spanish verb meaning to insert or put something in. It comes from the Latin 'insertare.' The example shows: 'I looked for spaces to insert words that would make sense.'
Huésped refers to a guest or lodger—someone staying at a place temporarily. The example shows: 'they offer a complimentary breakfast buffet to hotel guests.'
Calzar specifically refers to putting on or wearing shoes. The example shows: 'she used to wear my best clothes and wear shiny shoes.'
Humildad is a noun meaning humility—the quality of being humble and not proud. The example shows: 'She was a marvelous person who radiated peace and humility.'
Hilera refers to a row or line of things or people arranged in order. The example shows: 'they made the prisoners form a line and sing a chorus.'
Tenis is the Spanish word for the sport of tennis, played with a racket and ball on a court. The example shows: 'we used to play it with a tennis racket.'
Pato is a Spanish noun meaning duck—an aquatic waterfowl. The example shows: 'the aquatic birds, including ducks and geese.'
Enfermar is a verb meaning to become ill or sick. The example shows: 'because he became ill with arthritis, in its most aggressive form.'
Conversión is a noun meaning conversion or change from one state to another. The example shows: 'it was like a rebirth, or a second conversion.'

s: 'palpar' means 'to feel, touch, fondle' in Spanish
e: Palpar is a verb meaning to feel or touch something, often used in medical contexts. The example shows: 'the doctor is going to feel the joints around your jaw.'
Rojizo is an adjective meaning reddish or ruddy—having a tinge of red. The example shows: 'that circular ring tends to show a reddish color in the middle.'
Invocar is a verb meaning to invoke or call upon, often used in formal or mystical contexts. The example shows: 'he used to invoke the name of dead leaders.'

s: 'pulsar' means 'to press, beat, strike, throb' in Spanish
e: Pulsar is a verb meaning to press, beat, or throb. The example shows: 'You can add your comments and then press Send again.'
Impaciente is an adjective meaning impatient—not willing to wait or tolerant of delays. The example shows: 'The truth is that I'm very anxious, very impatient.'
Apunte is a noun meaning a note or annotation, commonly used for notes taken in class. The example shows: 'I need the notes you take in class.'
Llano is an adjective meaning flat or level. The example shows: 'the flat plateau of Patagonia.'
Sofisticado is an adjective meaning sophisticated—showing refined or advanced knowledge. The example shows: 'they have also developed sophisticated statistical models.'
Filtro is a noun meaning a filter or screening device. The example shows: 'Turn the filter valve to the WASH position.'
Primordial is an adjective meaning fundamental or primary—of first importance. The example shows: 'the mission was essential and secret.'
Inolvidable is an adjective meaning unforgettable—something that cannot be forgotten. The example shows: 'this unforgettable feeling of her kisses that he still felt.'
Plantilla is a noun with multiple meanings including template or stencil—a pattern to follow. The example shows: 'On that template we can make all the modifications.'
Espanto is a noun meaning fright or ghost—a sudden fear or a supernatural apparition. The example shows: 'the surprise and shock that this news will cause.'
Contratación is a noun meaning hiring or recruitment—the process of bringing people into employment. The example shows: 'the first requirement for hiring: professional experience.'
Harto is an adjective meaning full or sick of—having had enough of something. The example shows: 'I am fed up with Rene's complaints.'
Triángulo is a noun meaning triangle—a geometric shape with three sides and three angles. The example shows: 'he is lost in the Bermuda Triangle.'
Preventivo is an adjective meaning preventive or preemptive—designed to prevent something before it happens. The example shows: 'Preventive medicine depends on the participation of everyone.'
Impotencia is a noun meaning impotence—the state of being unable to do something or lacking power. The example shows: 'the bitter powerlessness of the loser was noticeable.'

s: This Spanish noun names a medical condition characterized by elevated blood glucose levels.
q: What Spanish word is related to "disease"?
e: Diabetes is a noun referring to the medical condition characterized by high blood sugar levels. The example shows: 'for those who suffer from diabetes, high blood pressure, or other illnesses.'
Semanal is an adjective meaning weekly—occurring once a week or every seven days. The example shows: 'normally edited on a daily or weekly basis.'
Tele is a colloquial Spanish noun for television or TV. The example shows: 'it was as easy as reading or watching television.'
Insuficiente is an adjective meaning insufficient—not enough or inadequate. The example shows: 'I would think that it was considered insufficient pay.'
Documental is an adjective meaning documentary—relating to or presented as fact without dramatization. The example shows: 'they presented a script for either fiction or a documentary.'

s: 'concurrir' means 'to coincide, concur, meet' in Spanish
e: Concurrir is a verb meaning to coincide, concur, or meet together. The example shows: 'the doctors participated during the operation.'
Vinculación is a noun meaning a link or connection between things or people. The example shows: 'they allow a reciprocal connection between the peoples of our country.'
Difunto is a noun/adjective meaning deceased—a person who has died. The example shows: 'crying and grieving, in memory of the deceased.'
Plenitud is a noun meaning fullness or abundance—the state of being complete or plentiful. The example shows: 'everyone was strong, in the fullness of life.'
Interrupción is a noun meaning an interruption—a pause or break in continuity. The example shows: 'I need a day without interruptions to do this project.'
Disgusto is a noun meaning disgust or annoyance—a feeling of strong dislike or irritation. The example shows: 'mom rejected the proposal with annoyance.'
Cómico is an adjective meaning comic—relating to or involving humor and comedy. The example shows: 'it combines the highbrow and the popular, the comic and the serious.'

s: 'entonar' means 'to intone, sing, modulate' in Spanish
e: Entonar is a verb meaning to intone, sing, or modulate one's voice. The example shows: 'the participants are beginning to sing the songs.'
Exhibición is a noun meaning an exhibition or showing—a public display of art, objects, or performances. The example shows: 'we visited the modern art exhibition.'
Reseña is a noun meaning a review or outline—a brief summary or critical evaluation. The example shows: 'a brief review of the history of the group.'

s: 'despegar' means 'to detach, take off (plane)' in Spanish
e: Despegar is a verb meaning to detach or take off—especially used for planes beginning flight. The example shows: 'many planes are able to take off even when filled with fuel.'
Disponibilidad is a noun meaning availability—the quality of being available or accessible. The example shows: 'the lack of availability of the system is the cause of the problems.'
Previsión is a noun meaning a forecast or precaution—advance planning or prediction of future events. The example shows: 'according to the sales forecast, we will receive bonuses.'
Implacable is an adjective meaning relentless or merciless—unable to be appeased or stopped. The example shows: 'the relentless tyranny troubled the whole country.'
Futbolista is a noun meaning a soccer player—someone who plays the sport of football (soccer). The example shows: 'the soccer player always wants to play and help his team.'
Marea is a noun meaning a wave, tide, or ebb—the rise and fall of ocean water or a large wave. The example shows: 'I prefer to go to the beach when the tide is low.'
Relevancia is a noun meaning relevance—the quality of being relevant or pertinent. The example shows: 'he wrote about some topic that was relevant to all humanity.'
Siniestro is an adjective meaning sinister—having a threatening or evil quality. The example shows: 'her suspicions would be confirmed on a sinister Tuesday afternoon.'

s: pillar means 'to capture, catch' in Spanish
e: Pillar is a Spanish verb meaning 'to capture' or 'catch.' Example: 'Pude pillar a Alejandro' means 'I was able to catch Alejandro.' It can also mean to catch someone in a mistake or by surprise.
Récord is a noun meaning a 'record' or 'best achievement in a field.' It's similar to English, as Spanish borrowed from English. Example: 'en tiempo récord' means 'in record time.'
